exemplar.title: How does salary cap regulations and financial strategies impact the financial stability and performance of large market v/s small market NBA teams?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does salary cap regulations and financial strategies impact the financial stability and performance of large market v/s small market NBA teams?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The context and scope are introduced clearly, with precise explanation of NBA salary-cap mechanisms and justification for team selection.
Research question is prominently stated and remains the organising thread throughout, demonstrating sustained focus.
Methodology shows an appropriate, well-informed selection of secondary sources and analytical frameworks, aligning directly with the investigationâs aims.
weaknesses: Minor inconsistency in the phrasing of the research question between introduction and conclusion.
Reliance exclusively on secondary data limits triangulation possibilities; the inclusion of primary stakeholder insights would strengthen the approach.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although a small wording inconsistency and lack of primary data slightly temper the methodological rigour, the clarity, focus and comprehensiveness of the investigation clearly meet the highest band descriptors. Awarding the maximum 6â/â6 reflects the studentâs accurate and effective communication of the topic, a well-focused research question, and evidence of informed methodological choice.

strengths: Source material is applied with clear relevanceâtables, graphs and valuation figures are seamlessly integrated and consistently cited.
Demonstrates coherent, structured subject knowledge of salary-cap economics, luxury tax, revenue sharing and business-management concepts.
Terminology (e.g., CPW, ROI, luxury tax) is used accurately and consistently throughout, supporting the analytical discourse.
weaknesses: A few clip-art images lack source attribution.
Occasional conceptual simplifications (e.g., treating the NBA as a âmonopolyâ) prevent an even deeper theoretical precision.
Minor formatting slips (random symbols, inconsistent superscripts) appear but do not undermine terminological accuracy.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs use of source material, depth of subject knowledge and consistent application of key terminology align fully with the top-band criteria. Despite small attribution and precision issues, the workâs overall strength justifies the full 6â/â6 score.

strengths: Research draws on over 30 current and directly relevant sources, demonstrating excellent breadth and consistent relevance to the research question.
Analysis is sophisticated: multi-year CPW calculations, valuation comparisons and tool-specific evaluations are effectively supported by data.
Arguments are well-reasoned and critically evaluated, comparing financial strategies with balanced appraisal of advantages and limitations.
weaknesses: Absence of primary research and limited exploration of causality in some analyses prevent an even more robust inquiry.
Some evaluative sections occasionally reiterate earlier points instead of introducing new critical insight.
The conclusion could be strengthened by reflecting on limitations and outlining avenues for future primary research.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The depth and sophistication of research, analysis and evaluation consistently meet the highest band descriptors. While a few refinements could further elevate the work, the investigationâs analytical rigour and critical reasoning merit the maximum 12â/â12.

strengths: All required elements of the EE structure are present and logically organised: title page, contents, sections, bibliography and appendices.
Tables and figures are labelled and integrated appropriately; pages are numbered and wordâcount requirements are met.
Logical flow within and between sections ensures readability and coherence.
weaknesses: Formatting glitches remain (misplaced superscripts, truncated URLs, low-resolution images).
Axes in some figures lack labels and units; the table of contents could better indicate sub-level indentations for navigability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report meets every structural requirement and maintains a coherent, reader-friendly layout, fully satisfying the top-band descriptors for Presentation. The remaining format-related imperfections are minor and do not detract from overall clarity, justifying the full 4â/â4 score.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated early and remains the organising thread throughout analysis, tools and conclusion. It is sufficiently narrow (two named teams, specified financial mechanisms) and clearly linked to the business-management concepts explored. Some inconsistency in wording (âfinancial stability and performanceâ vs âcompetitivenessâ) marginally reduces focus, so awarding 5 within the top band rather than full marks is appropriate.

Arguments compare the effectiveness of contrasting financial strategies and weigh advantages against limitations for both franchises, demonstrating critical evaluation. Conclusions are mostly well-supported by earlier evidence. However, evaluation sometimes reiterates earlier points and lacks explicit stakeholder perspectives or alternative viewpoints, so the work fits solidly but not eminently within the 7-9 band.

The essay demonstrates coherent understanding of salary-cap economics, luxury-tax structures and BM concepts (ratio analysis, cost-benefit, Porter, FFA). Explanations link theory to NBA specifics with fluency. Occasional simplifications (e.g., treating NBA as âmonopolyâ) prevent a flawless score but knowledge remains strong and effectively sourced.

Key terminologyâCPW, ROI, luxury tax, revenue sharingâis defined and then used consistently and accurately throughout the analysis. Minor formatting slips (random symbols, inconsistent superscripts) are present but terminological accuracy is unaffected, supporting a 5 in the top band.

The student performs multi-year CPW calculations, valuation comparisons and tool-specific evaluations that go well beyond description. Links between spending patterns and on-court outcomes are logically developed. Nonetheless, some analyses stop short of exploring causality in depth (e.g., Porter forces largely descriptive), so sophistication is good rather than exceptionalâwarranting 7 within the 7-9 band.

Tables, graphs and valuation figures are consistently integrated into the discussion and explicitly referenced in analysis sections (e.g., CPW calculations, valuation comparisons). Citations are provided for virtually every data set. A few images are generic clip-art without source attribution, but overall application remains clearly relevant and appropriate, meriting high-band credit.

A wide range of secondary data sources (Statista, Forbes, Spotrac, Basketball-Reference, scholarly article on incentives, etc.) is drawn on, and four analytical frameworks are selected purposefully. Evidence of informed selection is clearâdata sets align closely with each toolâs requirements. The sole limitation is exclusive reliance on secondary sources with no attempt to triangulate through primary stakeholder insights, so 5/6 is a defensible placement.

Research draws on more than 30 cited sources, incorporating financial databases, academic literature and league publications that are directly pertinent to the RQ. Data sets are current (through 2024/25) and tailored to each analytical tool, indicating good breadth and clear relevance; however, the absence of primary research and a few dubious popular-media sources preclude the highest 10-12 range.

Pages are numbered, tables and figures labelled, and citations mostly consistent. Word count is within limit. Nonetheless, there are persistent formatting glitches (misplaced superscripts, random symbols, truncated URLs, some low-resolution images) indicating partial but not complete adherence to layout conventions; the mid-band (1-2) is therefore appropriate.

The student introduces the context of NBA salary-cap rules clearly, specifies the two franchises under study and justifies their choice. Background information on market size, revenue, and historical performance is provided, giving readers an immediate and accurate grasp of the investigationâs scope. Explanations of key terms (salary-cap, luxury tax, revenue sharing) are embedded or placed in the glossary, ensuring comprehensive understanding. Minor lapsesâsuch as the duplicated/adjusted wording of the research question in the conclusionâdo not materially detract from otherwise effective and accurate communication, so the top band is warranted.

The essay follows required EE structure: title page with word count, abstract omitted per new guide, contents table, intro, methodology, main body, conclusion, bibliography and appendix. Logical flow within sections is clear. All major elements are present and coherent, meeting full-band expectations.

annotations.comment: Consider indenting sub-levels in the Table of Contents to improve navigability for readers.
Definitions of technical terms (e.g., âsalary capâ, âluxury taxâ) are clear and embedded in the text or glossary, supporting reader comprehension.
The title and research question are clearly presented, establishing an effective framework for the investigationâs focus.
The research question wording changes slightly between introduction and conclusion; unify it (e.g., âfinancial stability and performanceâ vs âcompetitivenessâ) for consistency.
The word count and session details are correctly placed on the title page, meeting IB formatting requirements.
The introduction situates the NBA well but opens with broad generalities; focus more quickly on the core financial dynamics you will analyse.
The rationale for selecting the Grizzlies and Warriors is well-argued, linking market size to strategic diversity.
Footnote placeholders often state âRefer to Glossaryâ without source context; include full citations to maintain academic rigor.
Methodology outlines a clear secondary-research approach, demonstrating informed selection of articles, databases and frameworks.
Reliance solely on secondary sources limits triangulation; consider primary data (e.g., management interviews) to strengthen your methodology.
The list of tools is comprehensive, linking each framework to a clear analytical purpose.
The revenue figures in Figs. 1 and 2 are well integrated, but neither axis nor units are labelled; add labels for clarity.
The narrative interpretation of revenue dips (e.g., COVID impact) demonstrates clear linkage between data and context.
The luxury tax table (Table 1) lacks commentary on the year-to-year trend; include a brief analysis to enhance depth.
Player salary tables are integrated well, but adding mean and variance statistics would strengthen quantitative evaluation.
Introduce the cost-per-win formula explicitly, e.g., $$	ext{CPW} =  rac{	ext{Player Salaries}}{	ext{Wins}}$$ to improve conceptual clarity.
The cost-per-win metric is a valuable insight into spending efficiency; its application shows originality and analytical depth.
In the force field analysis, justify the numerical weights (1â5) with criteria or data points to support your evaluation.
The Porterâs Five Forces diagram is used effectively, but would benefit from direct links to NBA case details in the caption.
Formatting inconsistencies appear in superscripts (e.g., random symbols); ensure uniform application throughout the document.
The conclusion revisits key tools but omits reflection on limitations and future primary research; include a reflective commentary to strengthen engagement.
The appendices are appropriately separated and labeled; placement after the bibliography is correct.
The glossary entries are clear, but some definitions (e.g., ROI) would benefit from formula inclusion for precision.
